title: uopract
text: uopract Activate a component operation. Explain this code int uopract (UHACT hOpr, long* pStatus, long* pProcErr); Copy Parameters hOpr The handle to the operation to be activated, as returned by uinstopr pStatus (Output) The activation status ( $status ). If you are not interested in this output, supply a zero value instead of a pointer. pProcErr (Output) The activation error status ( $procerror ). If you are not interested in this output, supply a zero value instead of a pointer. Return Values For more information, see the "Call-In API Return Codes" topic. Use Library: ucall Description With this function, you can activate an operation in an instantiated component, after you have obtained a handle to the operation by a call to uinstopr and (optionally) provided parameters. The function uopract is the 3GL equivalent of the 4GL activate ProcScript statement, and can return the same error codes. Note that, although the pStatus and pProcErr variables are optional, it is good programming practice to supply them, and to examine the status codes after return from uopract . If you do not use these parameters, you have no way of knowing whether your operation was successfully activated or what its return status was. Example: uecreate, uedelete, ufreeh, uinstdel, uinstnew, uinstopr, and uopract The following simple call-in program shows how to activate the EXEC operation of the service MYSVC. As usual, error checking has been omitted for the sake of brevity. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> void main (void) { UHACT hEnv; /* Uniface environment */ UHACT hIn; /* Instance handle */ UHACT hOp; /* Operation handle */ long opStat; /* Activate status return parameter */ /* Create the environment */ uecreate(1, 0, 0, "my.asn", 0, 0, &hEnv); /* Instantiate service MYSVC */ uinstnew(hEnv, "MYSVC", 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, 0); uinstopr(hIn, "EXEC", 0, &hOp); /* Instantiate the EXEC operation */ uopract (hOp, &opStat, 0); /* Activate the operation */ uinstdel(hIn, 0); /* Destroy the instance */ ufreeh (&hOp); /* Free the operation */ ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free the instance */ uedelete(hEnv, -1); /* Shut down the environment */ ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free the environment handle */ return 0; // on success } Copy Related Links Call-In API Return Codes Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise. uinstopr Return the handle to an operation. activate Calls an operation on a component instance.
